rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use subcomponent ports only in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections are defined
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope based on the target component or implementation
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when modifying the model
Use only visible identifiers that are in scope for the current annex block
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assign` only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
For output dependency contracts, write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless the model provides an explicit data value for the event
